editableNewDialogue.text: give me a short poem about music

Music slips through quiet air,
a silver thread you cannot see,
turning footsteps into prayer,
unlocking doors inside of me.

A drum can steady stormy nights,
a violin can warm the cold,
and in the chorus, scattered lights
become one story being told.
I’m not sure—“termogena” doesn’t appear to be a standard English word. It could be a misspelling, a name, or a term from another language. Where did you see it (song lyric, brand, caption)?
i saw it in a comment thread. also what is #WeLoveHate supposed to be?

Hashtags like #WeLoveHate are often used as campaign tags—either for a web series, a marketing push, or an ironic “love/hate” theme. Without the original post, it’s hard to pin down.

If you paste the surrounding text (or describe the post), I can help infer whether it’s a show title, a challenge, or just clickbait.

Unrelated trivia: the "Wilhelm scream" is a famous stock scream used in a lot of movies, and the first modern hashtag was popularized on Twitter in 2007.
